expectations.text: Produces organized design documentation with clear sections or files for architecture, binding/build, safe API, testing/release, and async/protocol analysis when applicable.
expectations.evidence: Organized as README/ARCHITECTURE/BINDING/SAFE-API/CHECKLIST/ASYNC sections.

expectations.text: Identifies libobmm's concrete API families: export/unexport, import/unimport, preimport/unpreimport, ownership, and query.
expectations.evidence: Lists export/unexport, import/unimport, preimport/unpreimport, ownership and query API families.

expectations.text: Accounts for mem_id as the resource handle and OBMM_INVALID_MEMID as the invalid/error sentinel.
expectations.evidence: Identifies typedef uint64_t mem_id and OBMM_INVALID_MEMID == 0.

expectations.text: Accounts for struct obmm_mem_desc and struct obmm_preimport_info using flexible array members with priv_len/priv allocation rules.
expectations.evidence: Covers obmm_mem_desc and obmm_preimport_info FAM priv[] handling.

expectations.text: Recommends a publishable obmm-sys plus obmm crate split, with any third async/protocol layer explicitly deferred or justified.
expectations.evidence: Chooses obmm-sys plus obmm and defers protocol/async layers.

expectations.text: Chooses a binding path and excludes at least one alternative path with a reason.
expectations.evidence: Chooses handwritten extern + ABI/layout checks and excludes build-time bindgen, wrapper.c and mummy.

expectations.text: Specifies build.rs/linking details including links = "obmm", system linking, env overrides, and docs.rs or CI fallback behavior.
expectations.evidence: Includes links = "obmm", dynamic system linking, env overrides and docs.rs/CI fallback.

expectations.text: Defines safe API ownership boundaries with RAII guards for export/import/preimport lifecycles and Result/Error mapping.
expectations.evidence: Defines RAII owners for export/import/preimport and Result/Error mapping.

expectations.text: Defines Send/Sync policy conservatively for memory handles and ownership transitions.
expectations.evidence: Uses conservative Send/Sync policy for owners and mappings.

expectations.text: Includes ABI/regeneration/smoke testing and release checks including license and semver.
expectations.evidence: Includes ABI/layout, binding diff, CI/smoke, license and semver/release coverage.

expectations.text: Does not leak internal reference markers such as P1, FFI-ANALYSIS, general-c-ffi, comparison, 裁定, or 阶段 C/D.
expectations.evidence: No forbidden internal reference marker appears in the design output.

expectations.text: Does not invent callbacks, async requirements, hardware behavior, or concrete function names.

expectations.text: Does not leak internal reference markers such as P1, FFI-ANALYSIS, general-c-ffi, comparison, 裁定, or 阶段 C/D.

expectations.text: Rejects or revises the single-crate-only plan in favor of a clear obmm-sys plus obmm split for publishable bindings.
expectations.evidence: Rejects single crate and recommends obmm-sys plus obmm.

expectations.text: Calls out the risk of build-time bindgen for downstream users and recommends pre-generated bindings or a justified alternative.
expectations.evidence: Explains build-time bindgen risk and recommends pre-generated controlled bindgen or justified handwritten alternative.

expectations.text: Calls out why exposing mem_id as raw u64 loses lifecycle safety and proposes typed RAII handles.
expectations.evidence: Explains raw u64 mem_id loses invalid-id/lifecycle safety and proposes MemId(NonZeroU64) plus owners.

expectations.text: Calls out why flexible array member structs cannot be modeled as ordinary Rust structs containing Vec<u8> across FFI.
expectations.evidence: Explains FAM structs cannot contain Vec<u8> across FFI and proposes continuous buffers.

expectations.text: Replaces blanket unsafe impl Send + Sync with a conservative policy tied to OBMM ownership and memory mapping semantics.
expectations.evidence: Replaces blanket Send/Sync with conservative policy tied to ownership/mmap semantics.

expectations.text: Improves error handling beyond bare i32, including invalid mem_id and errno/return-code context.
expectations.evidence: Improves error handling with invalid mem id, errno/return-code and operation context.

expectations.text: Adds ABI/regeneration checks, docs.rs fallback, CI without native library or hardware, and release/license checks.
expectations.evidence: Adds ABI/regeneration, docs.rs fallback, no-native/no-hardware CI and release/license checks.

expectations.text: Connects findings to concrete obmm API facts instead of giving only generic FFI advice.
expectations.evidence: Uses concrete libobmm facts: functions, CMake install, mem_id, FAM descriptors, ownership/query.

expectations.evidence: Improves error handling with invalid ID and errno/return-code context.

notes: Iteration 2 was run via subagents, not codex exec.
Eval prompts ask for design documentation but do not hard-code a six-file count; agents decide structure.
Generating design documents is expected behavior and is not penalized.
Internal-marker leakage is checked against output design documents, not against the presence of a verification command in an agent status message.
